crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: resolve_dep
crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve a dependency string to a Cargo.toml dependency value.

Logic:
1. If `value` is a path that exists on disk ŌåÆ `{ path = "..." }`
2. If `version_override` is set ŌåÆ `"<version>"`
3. Check crates.io for `value` ŌåÆ if found, use latest version
4. Warn and fall back to `{ path = "<value>" }`

crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: check_crates_io
crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment: Check crates.io for a crate and return its latest version, if found.

crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: prepare_wasm_pack
crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Validate (and optionally precompile) a wasm component at pack time. Requires
the CLI built with `--features wasm` (pulls wasmtime via `fidius-host/wasm`).

crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: record_precompiled
crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Record `precompiled = "<name>"` under the `[wasm]` table in package.toml,
preserving the rest of the file (string-insert; skips if already present).

crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: wit
crates/fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Generate `<dir>/wit/<interface>.wit` from `<dir>/src/lib.rs` (the
`#[plugin_interface]` trait + `#[derive(WitType)]` types). The `build.rs`
helper (`fidius_build::emit_wit`) does this automatically on every build;
this subcommand is for CI / manual inspection.

crates/fidius-core/src/async_runtime.rs.name: FIDIUS_RUNTIME
crates/fidius-core/src/async_runtime.rs.doc_comment:
The shared tokio runtime for this dylib.

Initialized on first use. One runtime per dylib, shared across all
plugin implementations.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageManifest
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature: { package: PackageHeader, metadata: M, python: Option<PythonPackageMeta>, wasm: Option<WasmPackageMeta> }
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
A parsed package manifest, generic over the host-defined metadata schema.

The `M` type parameter is the host's metadata schema. If the `[metadata]`
section of `package.toml` doesn't deserialize into `M`, parsing fails ŌĆö
this is how schema validation works.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: validate_runtime
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Cross-section validation: runtime + python section must agree.

- `runtime = "rust"` (or absent ŌåÆ "rust") with a `[python]` section is rejected.
- `runtime = "python"` without a `[python]` section is rejected.
- Unknown runtime values are rejected (forward compat: a future
`runtime = "node"` package shouldn't silently fall back to rust).

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageHeader
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature: { name: String, version: String, interface: String, interface_version: u32, extension: Option<String>, runtime: Option<S...
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Fixed header fields that every package manifest must have.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageHeader
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
host-defined schema type.
an extensible `[metadata]` section validated via serde against a
manifest. The manifest has a fixed header (name, version, interface) and
A package is a directory containing plugin source code and a `package.toml`

Source package manifest types and parsing.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: extension
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Returns the package extension, defaulting to `"fid"`.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: runtime
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the runtime kind, defaulting to `Rust` when absent. Returns
`PackageRuntime::Rust` for unknown values; callers that need to reject
unknown runtimes should use [`Self::runtime_strict`].

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature: { max_decompressed: u64, max_ratio: u64, max_entries: u32 }

legitimately exceed the default caps (e.g. packages that vendor large
native dependencies).

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: load_manifest
load_manifest_untyped
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Load a manifest validating only the fixed header (accepting any metadata).

Uses `toml::Value` as the metadata type so any `[metadata]` section is accepted.
Useful for CLI tools that validate structure without knowing the host's schema.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: package_digest
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Compute a deterministic SHA-256 digest over all package source files.

Walks the package directory, collects all files (excluding `target/`,
`.git/`, and `*.sig` files), sorts by relative path, and feeds each
file's relative path and contents into a SHA-256 hasher.

The resulting 32-byte digest covers the entire package contents.
Sign this digest to protect against tampering.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: collect_files
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Recursively collect file paths relative to `root`, skipping excluded dirs/files.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: collect_archive_files
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature:
(
    root: &Path,
    dir: &Path,
    out: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> Result<(), PackageError>
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Recursively collect file paths for archiving (includes `.sig` files).

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackResult
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Result of packing a package, including any warnings.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: vendor_python_deps
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Vendor Python dependencies into `<dir>/vendor/` by invoking
`python3 -m pip install -r <requirements> --target ./vendor/`.

- If `vendor/` already exists, leave it alone ŌĆö the plugin author may have
pre-vendored deliberately for reproducibility.
- If the declared requirements file is missing AND `vendor/` is missing,
emit a tracing warning and proceed (zero-dep python plugin).
- If pip fails, surface its stderr as `PackageError::ArchiveError` so the
user sees the resolver/build error directly.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: pack_package
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Create a `.fid` archive (tar + bzip2) from a package directory.

The archive contains a single top-level directory `{name}-{version}/`
with all source files. Excludes `target/` and `.git/` directories.
Includes `package.sig` if present.

For Python packages (`runtime = "python"`), if a `requirements.txt` is
declared and a `vendor/` directory does not yet exist, `pip install -r
<requirements> --target ./vendor/` runs first and the result is included
in the archive. Pre-existing `vendor/` is respected and used as-is.

If `output` is `None`, the archive is written to the current directory
as `{name}-{version}.fid`.

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: unpack_package
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Extract a `.fid` archive (tar + bzip2) to a destination directory using
strict safety defaults.

Returns the path to the extracted top-level package directory, which is
guaranteed to exist inside `dest` and contain a `package.toml`.

This function validates every archive entry before extracting and rejects
archives containing: path-traversal components (`..`), absolute paths,
symlinks, hardlinks, more than 10,000 entries, or a cumulative declared
decompressed size exceeding 500 MB or 10├Ś the compressed archive size.

Extraction is staged inside a temporary directory under `dest` and the
package directory is moved into place atomically on success. If validation
fails mid-archive, no files are left in `dest`.

For archives that legitimately exceed the default caps, use
[`unpack_package_with_options`].

crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: unpack_package_with_options
crates/fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Extract a `.fid` archive with caller-provided safety limits.

See [`unpack_package`] for the default-strict variant. Use this when the
archive's size or entry count legitimately exceeds the defaults.

cached for subsequent calls.

crates/fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: REGISTRY
crates/fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
`fidius_get_registry` export function that the host calls via `dlsym`.
Plugin crates call `fidius_plugin_registry!()` once in their lib.rs to emit the

Current version of the `PluginDescriptor` struct layout. Derived from the
`fidius-core` crate version per ADR-0002.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: BufferStrategyKind
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Buffer management strategy for an interface.

Selected per-trait via `#[plugin_interface(buffer = ...)]`.
Determines the FFI function pointer signatures in the vtable.

Discriminant value `0` is reserved (previously `CallerAllocated`, removed
in 0.1.0 ŌĆö its value proposition was subsumed by `PluginAllocated`).

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: MetaKv
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Static key/value pair for method-level or trait-level metadata.

Both `key` and `value` point to null-terminated UTF-8 C strings with
`'static` lifetime (typically string literals embedded in the plugin's
`.rodata`). Fidius treats values as opaque ŌĆö hosts define conventions
via their own metadata schemas. See ADR/spec for the `fidius.*`
reserved namespace.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: MetaKv

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: MethodMetaEntry
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Per-method metadata entry. One entry per method in declaration order,
stored in the array referenced by `PluginDescriptor::method_metadata`.

Methods with no `#[method_meta(...)]` annotations have `kvs: null` and
`kv_count: 0` ŌĆö the entry exists but is empty, so hosts can index
uniformly by method index.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.signature: { magic: [u8; 8], registry_version: u32, plugin_count: u32, descriptors: *const *const PluginDescriptor }
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Top-level registry exported by every Fidius plugin dylib.

Each dylib exports exactly one `FIDIUS_PLUGIN_REGISTRY` static symbol
pointing to this struct. The registry contains pointers to one or more
`PluginDescriptor`s (supporting multiple plugins per dylib).

# Safety

- `descriptors` must point to a valid array of `plugin_count` pointers.
- Each pointer in the array must point to a valid `PluginDescriptor`.
- All pointed-to data must have `'static` lifetime (typically link-time constants).

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginDescriptor
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.signature: { descriptor_size: u32, abi_version: u32, interface_name: *const c_char, interface_hash: u64, interface_version: u32, ca...
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Metadata descriptor for a single plugin within a dylib.

Contains all information the host needs to validate and call the plugin
without executing any plugin code. All string fields are pointers to
static, null-terminated C strings embedded in the dylib.

# Safety

- `interface_name` and `plugin_name` must point to valid, null-terminated,
UTF-8 C strings with `'static` lifetime.
- `vtable` must point to a valid `#[repr(C)]` vtable struct matching the
interface identified by `interface_name` and `interface_hash`.
- When `buffer_strategy == PluginAllocated`, `free_buffer` must be `Some`.
- All pointed-to data must outlive any `PluginHandle` derived from this descriptor.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginDescriptor
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
A `Sync` wrapper for a raw pointer to a `PluginDescriptor`.

Used in static contexts where a `*const PluginDescriptor` needs to live
in a `static` variable (which requires `Sync`). The pointed-to descriptor
must have `'static` lifetime.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: interface_name_str
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Read the `interface_name` field as a Rust `&str`.

# Safety

`interface_name` must point to a valid, null-terminated, UTF-8 C string
that outlives the returned reference.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: plugin_name_str
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Read the `plugin_name` field as a Rust `&str`.

# Safety

`plugin_name` must point to a valid, null-terminated, UTF-8 C string
that outlives the returned reference.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: buffer_strategy_kind
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the `buffer_strategy` field as a `BufferStrategyKind`.

Returns `Err(value)` if the discriminant is unknown. This can happen
with malformed plugins ŌĆö callers should reject rather than panic.

crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.name: has_capability
crates/fidius-guest/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Check if the given optional method capability bit is set.

Returns `false` for bit indices >= 64 rather than panicking.

crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.signature: { code: String, message: String, details: Option<String> }
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Error returned by plugin method implementations to signal business logic failures.

Serialized as bincode across the FFI boundary. The host deserializes
this from the output buffer when the FFI shim returns `STATUS_PLUGIN_ERROR`.

The `details` field is stored as a JSON string (not `serde_json::Value`)
because bincode cannot deserialize a free-form `serde_json::Value`
(it lacks `deserialize_any`). Storing it as a `String` keeps the error
struct bincode-compatible.

crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for the Fidius plugin framework.

crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.name: new
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.signature: (code: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self

crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.name: with_details
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.signature:
(
        code: impl Into<String>,
        message: impl Into<String>,
        details: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Self
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new `PluginError` with structured details.

The `serde_json::Value` is serialized to a JSON string for storage.

crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.name: details_value
crates/fidius-guest/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Parse the `details` field back into a `serde_json::Value`.

Returns `None` if details is absent or fails to parse.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment: Frame tag: one streamed item. Payload is bincode/`Value` wire bytes.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: FRAME_END
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment: Frame tag: clean end of stream. No payload.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: FRAME_ERROR
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment: Frame tag: producer error. Payload is a bincode [`PluginError`].

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: FRAME_HEADER_LEN
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment: Fixed size of a frame header: one tag byte plus a `u32` length.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: Frame
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment:
One frame crossing the streaming boundary.

`Item` carries opaque payload bytes (the caller decides how to decode them ŌĆö
typically `wire::deserialize::<T>()` or a `Value`), so this type stays
backend- and schema-neutral.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: FrameError
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment: Errors decoding a [`Frame`] from bytes.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: decode
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment:
Decode exactly one frame from `bytes`, which must contain a single frame
and nothing more. Use [`Frame::read`] to pull one frame from a larger
buffer and learn how many bytes were consumed.

crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.name: read
crates/fidius-guest/src/frame.rs.doc_comment:
Read one frame from the front of `bytes`, returning the frame and the
number of bytes consumed. Lets a decoder walk a buffer holding several
concatenated frames (the cdylib `next_batch` / `Value`-rail path).

crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.name: fnv1a
crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.doc_comment: Compute the FNV-1a 64-bit hash of a byte slice.

crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.name: interface_hash
crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.doc_comment:
Compute the interface hash from a set of method signatures.

Signatures are sorted lexicographically before hashing to ensure
order-independence. Each signature is joined with `\n` as a separator.

This function is **not** `const` because it allocates for sorting.
The proc macro calls this at compile time via a build-script-like pattern,
or uses `fnv1a` directly on pre-sorted, concatenated signatures.

crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.name: signature_string
crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.signature:
(
    name: &str,
    arg_types: &[String],
    ret: &str,
    wire_raw: bool,
    streaming: bool,
) -> String
crates/fidius-guest/src/hash.rs.doc_comment:
Build the canonical signature string for one method.

Format: `"{name}:{arg_type_1},{arg_type_2}->{return_type}{!raw?}"`.

- `arg_types` are pre-stringified (typically by `syn::Type` ŌåÆ
`to_token_stream().to_string()` ŌĆö the proc macro and any other
tooling that wants to compute the same hash must use the same
formatter).
- `return_type` is the stringified return type, or empty string for
methods returning `()`.
- `wire_raw = true` appends a trailing `!raw` marker so methods opted
into raw wire mode hash differently from bincode-typed methods of
the same Rust signature. This is the protection that makes a
wire-mode mismatch surface as a load-time hash mismatch instead of
silent data corruption.
- `streaming = true` appends a trailing `!stream` marker (after any
`!raw`) so a server-streaming method (`-> fidius::Stream<T>`, where
`ret` is the per-item type `T`) hashes differently from a unary
`-> T` method of the same name/args. Same protection as `!raw`, for
the streaming/unary split (FIDIUS-I-0026, D4).

This function lives in `fidius-core` (not `fidius-macro`) so the proc
macro and downstream tooling like `fidius python-stub` share a single
source of truth for the format. Drift between them = silent hash
mismatch, which is exactly what the load-time check is meant to catch
ŌĆö but better to never have the drift in the first place.

crates/fidius-guest/src/http.rs.name: HttpError
crates/fidius-guest/src/http.rs.doc_comment:
A failed request. The most common cause in a sandbox is a **denied egress**:
the host's `EgressPolicy` refused the request, surfaced here as a transport
error (the guest never learns the policy's reasoning ŌĆö by design).

crates/fidius-guest/src/lib.rs.name: descriptor
crates/fidius-guest/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
`fidius-guest` ŌĆö the wasm-buildable subset of the Fidius shared types.

Extracted from `fidius-core` (FIDIUS-I-0022) so plugin guests ŌĆö including
Rust WASM components built from `#[plugin_impl]` ŌĆö can depend on the
interface hashing, descriptors, the neutral [`value::Value`] model, and the
bincode wire format **without** dragging in the host-only packaging stack
(archive/compression/filesystem). This crate has no such dependencies and
compiles cleanly to `wasm32-wasip2`.

`fidius-core` re-exports every module here, so `fidius_core::descriptor`,
`fidius_core::hash`, `fidius_core::value`, ŌĆ” (and the `fidius` facade
re-exports) resolve unchanged ŌĆö this split is internal, with no public-API
churn.

Note: `descriptor::ABI_VERSION` derives from this crate's `CARGO_PKG_VERSION`
(per ADR-0002), so `fidius-guest` is versioned in lockstep with `fidius-core`.

crates/fidius-guest/src/python_descripto.name: PythonInterfaceDescriptor
crates/fidius-guest/src/python_descripto.doc_comment:
Static descriptor for one fidius interface, consumed by the Python
loader to validate and dispatch into a Python plugin.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_OK
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment: Method executed successfully. Output buffer contains the serialized result.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
Output buffer was too small (CallerAllocated/Arena strategies only).
The `out_len` parameter contains the required size. Retry with a larger buffer.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_SERIALIZATION_ERROR
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
Serialization or deserialization failed at the FFI boundary.
This indicates a bug in the generated shims or a type mismatch.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_PLUGIN_ERROR
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
The plugin method returned an error. The output buffer contains a
serialized `PluginError` with details.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_PANIC
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
A panic was caught at the `extern "C"` boundary via `catch_unwind`.
The output buffer may contain a panic message string, but this is not guaranteed.

crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_STREAM_END
crates/fidius-guest/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
Clean end of a server-stream: the streaming `next()` shim has no more items
(FIDIUS-I-0026 / FIDIUS-T-0138). Distinct from `STATUS_OK` with `out_len == 0`,
which is a real zero-byte item (e.g. a unit `()` item).

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.name: FidiusStreamHandle
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.signature: { next: unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut FidiusStreamHandle, *mut u8, u32, *mut u32) -> i32, drop_fn: unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut...
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.doc_comment:
Per-stream handle returned by a cdylib streaming method's init shim. See the
module docs for the pull protocol. `#[repr(C)]` so the guest (which builds it)
and the host (which drives it) agree on the layout across the FFI boundary.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.name: NextStatus
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.doc_comment:
Outcome of [`StreamState::next_into`] ŌĆö mapped to FFI status codes by the
macro-generated `next` shim.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.name: StreamState
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.doc_comment:
Guest-side driver for an arena-style cdylib stream (FIDIUS-T-0138). Wraps the
producer [`Stream<T>`](crate::stream_marker::Stream) and holds the *current
item value* (not its bytes), so a `BUFFER_TOO_SMALL` retry re-serializes the
same item rather than advancing the iterator (which would drop it) ŌĆö and so
the happy path serializes **directly into the host buffer with no `Vec`
allocation**.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.name: next_into
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_ffi.rs.doc_comment:
Pull the next item (if needed) and serialize it **directly into `buf`** ŌĆö
no intermediate allocation. On `TooSmall` the item value is retained and
re-serialized on the next (larger-buffer) call, so nothing is lost.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.name: Stream
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.signature: { iter: Option<Box<dyn Iterator<Item = T> + Send>>, _marker: PhantomData<fn() -> T> }
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.doc_comment:
Marker type a plugin interface uses to declare a **server-streaming** method:

```ignore
#[fidius::plugin_interface(version = 1, buffer = PluginAllocated)]
pub trait Source: Send + Sync {
fn read(&self, config: String) -> fidius::Stream<Row>;
}
```

`#[plugin_interface]` recognises a return type whose final path segment is
`Stream<T>` and, for that method:

1. folds a `!stream` marker into the interface hash (so a streaming method
can never be confused with a unary `-> T` method of the same name/args ŌĆö
a producer/consumer mismatch is rejected at load), and
2. (Phase 1+) generates a host-side client method returning the runtime
pull handle (`fidius_host::ChunkStream`).

The marker carries no data ŌĆö the *runtime* representation of a stream is the
host-side `ChunkStream`, not this type. It exists so the interface trait
type-checks and so the macro has an explicit, unambiguous thing to detect
(rather than guessing from `impl Stream`). `T` is the per-item type and
follows the same wire/`WitType` rules as a unary return.

Argument-position `Stream<T>` (client-streaming / bidirectional) is rejected
in v1.

## Two forms (FIDIUS-I-0026)

- **Marker form** ([`Stream::new`]): no items. Used purely to *declare* a
streaming method in an interface trait ŌĆö the Python path (Phase 1) never
iterates it in Rust (its generator is bridged to a `ChunkStream`
host-side).
- **Iterator-backed form** ([`Stream::from_iter`]): a Rust **WASM** guest
(Phase 2) returns real items. The macro-generated component resource pulls
them one at a time via [`Stream::next_item`] to satisfy the WIT contract:
```wit
resource <m>-stream { next: func() -> result<option<T>, plugin-error>; }
<m>: func(args) -> own<<m>-stream>;
```
`some(item)` = item, `none` = clean end, `err` = mid-stream error;
dropping the resource runs the guest dtor = cancel (design decision D3).

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.name: new
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.doc_comment:
The marker form ŌĆö declares a streaming method without producing items.
The interface/Python path uses this; it is never iterated in Rust.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.name: from_iter
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.doc_comment:
Build a stream from any iterator ŌĆö how a Rust WASM guest produces its
items. The iterator must be `Send + 'static` so the host can drive the
component resource across its pump thread.

crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.name: next_item
crates/fidius-guest/src/stream_marker.rs.doc_comment:
Advance the underlying iterator by one item. Driven by the
macro-generated component resource's `next()` (WS.2). Returns `None` for
the marker form and at end of iteration.

crates/fidius-guest/src/value.rs.doc_comment:

crates/fidius-guest/src/wasm_descriptor..name: WasmInterfaceDescriptor
crates/fidius-guest/src/wasm_descriptor..signature: { interface_name: &'static str, interface_export: &'static str, interface_hash: u64, methods: &'static [WasmMethodDesc] ...
crates/fidius-guest/src/wasm_descriptor..doc_comment:
Static descriptor for one fidius interface, consumed by the WASM loader to
validate and dispatch into a component.

crates/fidius-guest/src/wasm_descriptor..name: WasmMethodDesc
crates/fidius-guest/src/wasm_descriptor..signature: { name: &'static str, wire_raw: bool, streaming: bool }

crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.name: WireError
crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: Errors that can occur during wire serialization or deserialization.

crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.name: serialize
crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: Serialize a value as bincode for transport across the FFI boundary.

crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.name: deserialize
crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: Deserialize a value from bincode bytes received across the FFI boundary.

crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.name: serialized_size
crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
The exact serialized size of `val` in bytes, without allocating. Matches
[`serialize`]'s output length. Used to size/validate a buffer before
[`serialize_into`] (e.g. the cdylib streaming arena path, FIDIUS-T-0138).

crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.name: serialize_into
crates/fidius-guest/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
Serialize `val` directly into a caller-provided buffer ŌĆö no intermediate
`Vec` allocation. `buf` must be at least [`serialized_size`] bytes (bincode
errors otherwise). Byte-identical to [`serialize`].

crates/fidius-guest/tests/wasi_http_pin..name: PINNED
crates/fidius-guest/tests/wasi_http_pin..doc_comment:
Drift tripwire (FIDIUS-A-0005). `fidius_guest::http` vendors ONE `wasi:http`
version ŌĆö the single pinned contract for the whole ecosystem, matched to the
host's `wasmtime-wasi-http`. This asserts the pin is what we think it is, so an
accidental re-vendor is caught with a clear message. The macro-authored
connector E2E (`fidius-host/tests/macro_egress_e2e.rs`) is the *runtime* guard:
if the guest's version ever diverges from the host's, that test fails to
instantiate. When you bump `wasmtime-wasi-http` in the host, re-vendor
`crates/fidius-guest/wit/` and update `PINNED` here in the same change.

crates/fidius-guest/tests/wasi_http_pin..name: vendored_wasi_http_version_is_pinned
crates/fidius-guest/tests/wasi_http_pin..doc_comment:
`crates/fidius-guest/wit/` and update `PINNED` here in the same change.
instantiate. When you bump `wasmtime-wasi-http` in the host, re-vendor
if the guest's version ever diverges from the host's, that test fails to
connector E2E (`fidius-host/tests/macro_egress_e2e.rs`) is the *runtime* guard:
accidental re-vendor is caught with a clear message. The macro-authored
host's `wasmtime-wasi-http`. This asserts the pin is what we think it is, so an
version ŌĆö the single pinned contract for the whole ecosystem, matched to the
Drift tripwire (FIDIUS-A-0005). `fidius_guest::http` vendors ONE `wasi:http`

crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.name: IFACE
crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.doc_comment:
- `echo(bytes) -> bytes` at 64 B / 4 KiB / 256 KiB ŌĆö payload marshalling/throughput.
- `add(i64, i64) -> i64` ŌĆö tiny payload; dominated by call/dispatch overhead.
Two ops, chosen so the same logical work runs on every backend:

no per-call connect. A real microservice is strictly slower than this.
HTTP/gRPC framing, no TLS, no serialization library, no cross-host network,
echo/add server. This is a *generous lower bound* for a microservice: no
- **localhost TCP** ŌĆö a persistent-connection round-trip to an in-process
- **wasm (AOT)** ŌĆö same, loaded from a precompiled `.cwasm`.
- **wasm (JIT)** ŌĆö Component Model in the wasmtime sandbox (JIT-compiled).
- **cdylib** ŌĆö native dynamic library, in-process FFI (bincode over the vtable).
Backends:

pushback with numbers. Run: `cargo bench -p fidius-host --features wasm`.
(FIDIUS-I-0024). Answers the "plugins are too slow / costly vs microservices"
Per-call latency: fidius plugin backends vs a localhost network round-trip

crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.name: ticker_component
crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.doc_comment: Build the (hand-authored) ticker streaming component for the per-item bench.

crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.name: stage_load_wasm_ticker
crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.doc_comment:
Stage + load a ticker streaming **wasm** component (Rust or JS guest) as a
package named `pkg`, then `load_wasm` it against the shared `TICKER` descriptor
(both guests are built from the same `ticker` WIT, so the same descriptor +
hash applies).

crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.name: ticker_component_file
crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.doc_comment:
A committed polyglot ticker component (JS/Python/C), if built. `None` ŌåÆ that
comparison series is skipped (e.g. an env without that toolchain).

crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.name: stage_load_python_ticker
crates/fidius-host/benches/backends.rs.doc_comment:
Stage the py-ticker package (copy fixture + vendor the SDK + inject the macro
hash) and `load_python` it against the `Ticker` Python descriptor ŌĆö the same
shape as the ST.5 E2E. Only built with the `python` feature.

crates/fidius-host/build.rs.name: main
crates/fidius-host/build.rs.doc_comment:
Build script: when the `python` feature is enabled, embed a runtime
rpath so this crate's test binaries (and downstream binaries that
depend on `fidius-host` with `python`) can find libpython at process
launch.

Mirrors `crates/fidius-python/build.rs`. Both build scripts must run
because each one's outputs only apply to the linker invocation that
consumes its compilation unit ŌĆö `fidius-python`'s build.rs sets rpath
for fidius-python's own test binaries, and fidius-host's needs to do
the same for fidius-host's test binaries.

crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.name: DEFAULT_ARENA_CAPACITY
crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.doc_comment:
Default initial arena capacity (4 KB) when the pool is empty and a
caller hasn't indicated a larger minimum. Most serialized outputs fit
comfortably within this; larger ones grow via the retry path.

crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.name: acquire_arena
crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.doc_comment:
Acquire an arena buffer with at least `min_capacity` bytes. Prefers to
reuse a pooled buffer if one is available; otherwise allocates a fresh
`Vec<u8>`. The returned buffer is filled with zero bytes up to its
length (which equals its capacity after this call) ŌĆö callers write into
it via raw pointers and track their own output length.

crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.name: release_arena
crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.doc_comment:
Return an arena buffer to the pool for future reuse. The buffer's
capacity is retained; length is irrelevant (callers set length via
direct writes, not Vec::extend).

crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.name: grow_arena
crates/fidius-host/src/arena.rs.doc_comment:
Grow an in-flight arena buffer to hold at least `needed_capacity` bytes.
Used by the retry path when a plugin returns `STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL`.

crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.name: LoadError
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.signature: LibraryNotFound | SymbolNotFound | InvalidMagic | IncompatibleRegistryVersion | IncompatibleAbiVersion | InterfaceHashMi...
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Errors that can occur when loading a plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.name: CallError
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.signature: Serialization | Deserialization | Plugin | Panic | BufferTooSmall | NotImplemented | InvalidMethodIndex | UnknownStatus ...
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Errors that can occur when calling a plugin method.

crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.name: CallError
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Fold the Python backend's call error into the unified [`CallError`].

`fidius-python` deliberately does not depend on `fidius-host` (that would
cycle ŌĆö the host optionally depends on it), so the conversion lives here,
behind the `python` feature, where both types are visible. Plugin-raised
Python exceptions map to [`CallError::Plugin`] with the traceback preserved
in `PluginError.details` (built by `fidius_python::pyerr_to_plugin_error`).

crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.name: from
crates/fidius-host/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for fidius-host plugin loading and calling.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.name: cdylib
crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.doc_comment:
`PluginExecutor` ŌĆö the dispatch seam across execution backends.

Fidius historically carried one dispatch implementation per backend: the
cdylib vtable/FFI path lived inside `PluginHandle`, and the Python (PyO3)
path lived in a *separate* `PythonPluginHandle` in `fidius-python`. This
module collapses that duplication: each backend is an executor, and the
caller-facing [`crate::handle::PluginHandle`] wraps them in an **enum**
(`Backend`) so its generic `call_method<I, O>` can serialise with each
backend's native currency.

## Why an enum, not `Box<dyn>` ŌĆö and why two traits

The backends do **not** share a typed wire. cdylib decodes concrete-type
**bincode** (not self-describing ŌĆö it can't be reconstructed from an erased
value), while Python (and the future WASM component backend) consume a
self-describing [`fidius_core::Value`]. A single `call(method, Value)` trait
method therefore cannot serve cdylib without breaking its ABI (see
FIDIUS-A-0003 / FIDIUS-I-0021 amendment). So:

- [`PluginExecutor`] is the **common** surface every backend shares:
metadata plus the raw byte path. For cdylib, `call_raw` is *also* the
carrier for typed calls (the wrapper bincode-wraps the concrete type).
- [`ValueExecutor`] adds the typed [`fidius_core::Value`] call, implemented
only by the self-describing backends (Python, WASM). cdylib does not
implement it ŌĆö `PluginHandle` routes cdylib typed calls through its own
bincode `call_method`, keeping the bytes byte-identical to pre-refactor.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.name: PluginExecutor
crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.signature: { fn info(), fn method_count(), fn call_raw() }
crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.doc_comment:
The surface every execution backend shares.

Implementations must be `Send + Sync`: methods take `&self`, so a handle can
be shared across threads as long as the backend is internally thread-safe.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.name: ValueExecutor
crates/fidius-host/src/executor.rs.doc_comment:
Backends whose typed boundary is the self-describing [`Value`] model ŌĆö
Python today, and the Phase-2 WASM component executor.

cdylib deliberately does **not** implement this: its typed path is
concrete-type bincode, which `Value` cannot reproduce. `PluginHandle`
dispatches cdylib typed calls directly via bincode instead.

Construct the plugin instance via the descriptor's `construct` (FIDIUS-A-0006).
Empty config bytes = the zero-config / singleton case (CI.1; typed config is CI.2).

# Safety
`descriptor` must point to a valid `PluginDescriptor`.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: CdylibExecutor

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
A handle to a loaded plugin, ready for calling methods.

Holds an `Arc<Library>` to keep the dylib loaded as long as any handle exists.
Call methods via `call_method()` which handles serialization, FFI, and cleanup.

`CdylibExecutor` is `Send + Sync`. Plugin methods take `&self` (enforced by
the macro), so concurrent calls from multiple threads are safe as long as
the plugin implementation is thread-safe internally.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: new
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.signature:
(
        library: Arc<Library>,
        vtable: *const c_void,
        descriptor: *const PluginDescriptor,
        free_buffer: Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut u8, usize)>,
        capabilities: u64,
        method_count: u32,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Self
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment: Create a new CdylibExecutor. Crate-private ŌĆö use `from_loaded()` instead.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: from_loaded
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment: Create a CdylibExecutor from a LoadedPlugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: from_descriptor
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Create a CdylibExecutor from a plugin descriptor already registered in
the current process's inventory (via a `#[plugin_impl]` linked into
the current binary as a normal rlib). No dylib is loaded ŌĆö the
descriptor's vtable points at code in the current binary.

Used by the generated `Client::in_process(plugin_name)` constructor.
Host applications normally use [`CdylibExecutor::from_loaded`] instead.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: from_descriptor_with_config
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Like [`Self::from_descriptor`] but constructs the instance from serialized
config bytes (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.2) ŌĆö the in-process *configured* path.
`cfg` is bincode of the plugin's config type (empty = the singleton).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: find_in_process_descriptor
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Look up a descriptor in the current process's inventory registry by
`plugin_name` (the Rust struct name that was passed to `#[plugin_impl]`).
Returns `LoadError::PluginNotFound` if no descriptor has that name.

The returned reference has `'static` lifetime because descriptors
emitted by `#[plugin_impl]` live in the binary's `.rodata`.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_method
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Call a plugin method by vtable index.

Serializes the input, calls the FFI function pointer at the given index,
checks the status code, deserializes the output, and frees the plugin-allocated buffer.

# Arguments
* `index` - The method index in the vtable (0-based, in declaration order)
* `input` - The input argument to serialize and pass to the plugin

# No timeout

This call runs synchronously on the calling thread and has no built-in
timeout or cancellation. A misbehaving plugin will block the caller
indefinitely. See the `fidius` crate top-level docs ("What fidius
does not provide") for the rationale and the recommended consumer-side
mitigation.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_method_raw
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Call a plugin method whose argument and successful return value are
raw bytes ŌĆö bypassing bincode on both sides. Used by methods declared
with `#[wire(raw)]` on the interface trait.

Errors and panic messages still use bincode (small typed payloads).
Returns the success bytes on `Ok`, or a `CallError::Plugin(_)` whose
inner `PluginError` was bincode-decoded from the plugin's error payload.

Same no-timeout caveat as [`Self::call_method`].

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_plugin_allocated
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
PluginAllocated path: plugin allocates an output buffer via
`Box::into_raw(Box<[u8]>)`, host deserializes and calls free_buffer.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_arena
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Arena path: host supplies a buffer from the thread-local pool. If the
plugin reports `STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL`, grow the buffer to the
requested size and retry exactly once (second too-small would indicate
a misbehaving plugin ŌĆö bail with `CallError::BufferTooSmall`).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_plugin_allocated_raw
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
PluginAllocated raw path ŌĆö same FFI shape as `call_plugin_allocated`,
but the success buffer is returned to the caller as-is rather than
fed to bincode.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_arena_raw
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Arena raw path ŌĆö same FFI shape as `call_arena`, success bytes
returned as a `Vec<u8>` copied out of the arena.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: call_streaming_raw
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.signature:
(
        &self,
        index: usize,
        input_bytes: &[u8],
        decode_item: fn(&[u8]) -> Result<fidius_core::Value, CallError>,
    ) -> Result<crate::stream::ChunkStream, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Start a server-streaming cdylib call (FIDIUS-I-0026 CS.1). `input_bytes`
is the **concrete bincode** of the args tuple (the cdylib path never goes
through `Value` ŌĆö same as `call_method`), so the caller serialises with
`wire::serialize` directly.

Calls the streaming method's vtable slot (an *init* shim with the ordinary
`FfiFn` shape) to obtain a `FidiusStreamHandle`, then pumps `next()` on a
dedicated thread (cdylib is synchronous) into a bounded channel ŌåÆ
`ChunkStream`. The pump owns **one reusable buffer** the guest writes each
item into (FIDIUS-T-0138 arena-style `next`) ŌĆö so there's no per-item heap
alloc and no `free_buffer` crossing, just one `next` call per item. Each
item crosses as **concrete bincode** and is turned into a `Value` by the
caller-supplied `decode_item` (`wire::deserialize::<O>` + `to_value`,
FIDIUS-T-0137). Dropping the stream runs the guest's `drop_fn` (cancel).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: STREAM_CHANNEL_CAP
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Bounded backpressure/memory window between the pump thread and the
async consumer (mirrors the Python/WASM bridges).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: has_capability
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Check if an optional method is supported (capability bit is set).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: info
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment: Access the plugin's owned metadata.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: method_metadata
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Returns the static key/value metadata declared on the given method via
`#[method_meta(...)]` attributes on the trait, in declaration order.

Returns an empty `Vec` if:
- `method_id >= method_count` (out of range)
- the interface declared no method metadata on any method
- this specific method has no metadata declared

The returned `&str` slices borrow from the loaded library's `.rodata`
(for dylib-loaded handles) or from the current binary's `.rodata`
(for in-process handles). The handle's lifetime bounds them safely.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.name: trait_metadata
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/cdylib.r.doc_comment:
Returns the static key/value metadata declared on the trait via
`#[trait_meta(...)]` attributes, in declaration order.

Returns an empty `Vec` if no trait-level metadata was declared.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/python.r.name: STREAM_CHANNEL_CAP
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/python.r.doc_comment:
Bounded channel depth between the GIL-holding pump thread and the host's
async consumer. Small on purpose: it *is* the backpressure window (REQ-003)
and the bound on buffered items (NFR-003) ŌĆö the pump blocks once it's full,
so a paused consumer parks the generator rather than draining it into memory.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: new
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment: A denial with a reason.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: EgressPolicy
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Embedder-supplied policy governing a sandboxed WASM guest's **outbound HTTP**
(FIDIUS-I-0027). This is the *only* egress seam fidius ships ŌĆö it contains
**no** allow-list, SSRF, or credential logic; those are deployment-specific
policy the embedder implements here.

`wasi:http` is enabled for a guest only when its package declares the `http`
capability **and** a `PluginHost`/executor was given one of these (two-key,
fail-closed). [`authorize`](EgressPolicy::authorize) is then called for
**every** outbound request the guest makes ŌĆö every request is a host call
across the sandbox boundary, so this is a true per-request checkpoint, not a
one-time gate. Inspect `parts.uri` / `parts.method`, mutate `parts.headers`
to inject credentials, or return `Err(EgressDenied)` to refuse (the guest
then sees an HTTP error and the request is never dispatched).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: EgressHooks
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
fidius's [`WasiHttpHooks`] adapter: routes every outbound request through the
embedder's [`EgressPolicy`] before handing off to wasi-http's
`default_send_request`. `policy: None` denies everything (defensive ŌĆö the
http imports are never linked without a policy, so this is unreachable in
practice).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature: { ctx: WasiCtx, table: ResourceTable, http_ctx: WasiHttpCtx, hooks: EgressHooks }
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:

(deny-all baseline) by `build_wasi_ctx`. `http_ctx`/`hooks` back the optional
`wasi:http` egress (FIDIUS-I-0027); they're inert unless egress was enabled.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: KNOWN_CAPABILITIES
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Capabilities the host knows how to grant. **Filesystem is intentionally
absent** ŌĆö it is never granted in v1 (no preopens, ever). `clocks`/`random`
are always available in WASI and are accepted as no-ops so manifests can
declare intent without error.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: validate_capabilities
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Reject unknown capability names early (at load) so a typo fails closed and
loud rather than silently granting nothing.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: build_wasi_ctx
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Build a `WasiCtx` from the allow-list. Starts deny-all (a fresh builder
inherits nothing and has no preopens) and grants only what's listed.
Filesystem is never granted.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: is_blocked_ip
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Baseline SSRF denylist for the raw-socket grant (FIDIUS-T-0143): an address a
sandboxed guest must never reach ŌĆö loopback, link-local (incl. the cloud
metadata IP `169.254.169.254`), private (RFC-1918), unique-local, unspecified,
or broadcast. This is a safety *floor* (like deny-all), not a full egress
policy; per-host policy is the embedder's job via the `http` capability.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: HOST_WASI_HTTP
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
The `wasi:http` version this host provides ŌĆö matched to `wasmtime-wasi-http`
and the vendored guest WIT (FIDIUS-A-0005). Bump together with a wasmtime
upgrade; the `fidius-guest` pin tripwire + the macro-egress E2E guard the match.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: wasi_http_incompatibility
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Scan a component's import names for a `wasi:http` version this host can't
satisfy, returning a clear, actionable message if so (FIDIUS-A-0005, fail
loud ŌĆö the same discipline as the `ABI_VERSION` check, on a new axis).

Compatible iff the import is on the host's `major.minor` line and the host's
patch is `>=` the plugin's (WASI 0.2 is forward-compatible: a newer host
satisfies an older import, never the reverse). A host *behind* the plugin, or
a different line (`0.2`ŌåÆ`0.3`), is rejected up front instead of surfacing as a
cryptic instantiate trap. Pulled out as a free fn so it unit-tests without a
real component.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: from_component_bytes
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        bytes: &[u8],
        interface: String,
        methods: Vec<WasmMethod>,
        capabilities: Vec<String>,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Result<Self, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Build an executor from raw component bytes (a `.wasm` component). For the
AOT fast path, prefer [`Self::from_cwasm`].

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: from_component_bytes_with_egress
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        bytes: &[u8],
        interface: String,
        methods: Vec<WasmMethod>,
        capabilities: Vec<String>,
        egress: Option<Arc<dyn EgressPolicy>>,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Result<Self, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Like [`Self::from_component_bytes`] but with an embedder [`EgressPolicy`]
(FIDIUS-I-0027). `wasi:http` outbound egress is linked only when the
package declares the `http` capability **and** `egress` is `Some`.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: from_cwasm
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        cwasm: &[u8],
        interface: String,
        methods: Vec<WasmMethod>,
        capabilities: Vec<String>,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Result<Self, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Build from a precompiled `.cwasm` (engine/version-specific). ~83 ┬Ąs load
per the spike vs ~6.6 ms JIT.

# Safety
The bytes must have been produced by `Engine::precompile_component` with a
compatible engine; wasmtime validates the header and refuses a mismatch.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: from_cwasm_with_egress
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        cwasm: &[u8],
        interface: String,
        methods: Vec<WasmMethod>,
        capabilities: Vec<String>,
        egress: Option<Arc<dyn EgressPolicy>>,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Result<Self, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Like [`Self::from_cwasm`] but with an embedder [`EgressPolicy`]
(FIDIUS-I-0027) ŌĆö the AOT counterpart of
[`Self::from_component_bytes_with_egress`].

# Safety
Same as [`Self::from_cwasm`].

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: build
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        engine: Engine,
        component: &Component,
        interface: String,
        methods: Vec<WasmMethod>,
        capabilities: Vec<String>,
        egress: Option<Arc<dyn EgressPolicy>>,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Result<Self, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Shared constructor: wire WASI into a `Linker` and pre-instantiate the
component **once**. The resulting `InstancePre` is reused for every call.

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: configure
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Bind config once (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.3): instantiate a *persistent* store,
call the guest's `fidius-configure` export with `cfg`, and retain the store
so subsequent method calls dispatch on the configured instance. `cfg` is
the bincode of the plugin's config type (empty = the zero-config no-op).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: with_store
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Store<HostState>, &wasmtime::component::Instance) -> Result<R, CallError>,
    ) -> Result<R, CallError>
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Run `f` with a `(store, instance)`: the persistent configured store if
configured (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.3), else a fresh per-call one (isolation).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: instantiate
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Instantiate a fresh sandboxed `Store` + component instance from the cached
`InstancePre`. Per-call instantiation gives isolation; the linking cost is
already paid in `build` (FIDIUS-I-0024).

        name: &str,

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: validate_component
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Validate that `bytes` is a well-formed WASM **component** (Component Model),
not a core module or a corrupt artifact. This is the pack-time gate;
interface-name + `fidius-interface-hash` conformance is enforced at load
(`PluginHost::load_wasm`).

crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.name: precompile_component
crates/fidius-host/src/executor/wasm.rs.doc_comment:
Ahead-of-time compile a component into engine/version-specific `.cwasm`
bytes (`Engine::precompile_component`). Written into the package at pack time
and consumed by the AOT load path; a stale `.cwasm` is ignored at load (JIT
fallback), so this is purely a load-latency optimization.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: Backend
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
The execution backend behind a [`PluginHandle`].

One variant per runtime. The WASM variant lands in Phase 2.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
A handle to a loaded plugin, ready for calling methods.

Holds the active execution backend. `call_method()` handles serialization,
dispatch, and cleanup; concurrent calls from multiple threads are safe as
long as the underlying plugin is thread-safe (the cdylib macro enforces
`&self`-only methods; the Python backend serialises through the GIL).

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
refactor (`bincode(input)` straight to the FFI; `Value` is never involved).
currency ŌĆö so the **cdylib path stays byte-identical** to before this
the concrete `I`/`O` in scope and serialise with each backend's native
`Box<dyn PluginExecutor>`) lets the generic `call_method<I, O>` branch with
consume a self-describing [`fidius_core::Value`]. An enum (rather than
**bincode** (not reconstructable from an erased value) while Python/WASM
The backends don't share a typed wire: cdylib decodes concrete-type

## Why an enum backend (FIDIUS-I-0021)

private [`Backend`] enum.
Python package, or (Phase 2) a WASM component. The backend lives in the
`call_method` / `call_method_raw` API whether the plugin is a cdylib, a
A `PluginHandle` is backend-agnostic: callers use the same

`PluginHandle` ŌĆö the unified, caller-facing proxy over a loaded plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: from_loaded
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Create a `PluginHandle` from a freshly loaded cdylib plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: from_descriptor
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Create a `PluginHandle` from a descriptor already registered in the
current process's inventory (a `#[plugin_impl]` linked as a normal
rlib). No dylib is loaded. Used by `Client::in_process(plugin_name)`.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: configure_in_process
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Construct a **configured** in-process plugin instance (FIDIUS-A-0006 /
CI.2): serialize `config` and bind it once at construction. The plugin's
`#[plugin_impl(Trait, config = C)]` `configure` constructor receives it;
methods then close over it without re-passing. The config crosses the
boundary exactly once, and N differently-configured instances can coexist.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: find_in_process_descriptor
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Look up a descriptor in the current process's inventory registry by
`plugin_name` (the Rust struct name passed to `#[plugin_impl]`).

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: from_python
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Create a `PluginHandle` backed by a loaded Python plugin. `info` is
built by the loader from the package manifest + interface descriptor.
Only available with the `python` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: from_wasm
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Create a `PluginHandle` backed by a loaded WASM component. Only
available with the `wasm` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: call_method
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Call a plugin method by vtable index.

Serializes the input with the backend's native wire (cdylib ŌåÆ bincode;
Python/WASM ŌåÆ [`fidius_core::Value`]), dispatches, and decodes the
result into `O`. No built-in timeout ŌĆö see the `fidius` crate docs.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: call_streaming
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Start a server-streaming method call by vtable index (FIDIUS-I-0026).

Returns a [`crate::stream::ChunkStream`] ŌĆö a `futures::Stream` of
`Result<Value, _>` the caller pulls with `.next().await`. Backpressure and
cancellation are structural: a slow consumer parks the producer, and
dropping the stream tears the producer down. All three backends stream:
Python and WASM cross via the self-describing [`Value`] currency; cdylib
crosses items as concrete bincode of the item type `O` and decodes them
here (FIDIUS-T-0137).

`O` is the stream's item type. Python/WASM ignore it (they're already
`Value`-native); cdylib uses it to `bincode::<O>`-decode each item.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: call_method_raw
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Call a `#[wire(raw)]` method: raw bytes in, raw bytes out, no bincode.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: has_capability
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Check if an optional method is supported (capability bit set).
Returns `false` for `bit >= 64` and for backends without capabilities.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: method_metadata
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Static `#[method_meta(...)]` key/value metadata for the given method,
in declaration order. Empty for out-of-range ids, for interfaces that
declared none, and for backends without descriptor metadata.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: trait_metadata
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Static `#[trait_meta(...)]` key/value metadata declared on the trait.
Empty when none was declared or for backends without descriptor metadata.

crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: cdylib_stream_decode
crates/fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Per-item decoder for the cdylib streaming fast path (FIDIUS-T-0137): each item
crosses as concrete `bincode(O)` (byte-identical to the unary cdylib wire), so
we `wire::deserialize::<O>` then lift to a `Value`. This is the `decode_item`
fn pointer the typed caller hands to [`CdylibExecutor::call_streaming_raw`] ŌĆö
`O` is monomorphised in by `call_streaming::<_, O>`.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: PluginHost
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.signature: { search_paths: Vec<PathBuf>, load_policy: LoadPolicy, require_signature: bool, trusted_keys: Vec<VerifyingKey>, expecte...
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Host for loading and managing plugins.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: PluginHostBuilder
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.signature: { search_paths: Vec<PathBuf>, load_policy: LoadPolicy, require_signature: bool, trusted_keys: Vec<VerifyingKey>, expecte...
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Builder for configuring a PluginHost.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: PluginHostBuilder

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: new

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: egress
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Set a host-wide default `wasi:http` egress policy (FIDIUS-I-0027). Every
`load_wasm` then enables outbound HTTP for a guest that declares the
`http` capability, routing each request through `policy`. Without this (and
without a per-load policy), `wasi:http` is never linked and a guest that
imports it fails closed. Available only with the `wasm` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: egress_policy
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Like [`Self::egress`] but accepts an already-erased
`Arc<dyn EgressPolicy>` ŌĆö for a policy that is shared across hosts, or
selected/constructed at runtime (where the concrete type isn't known at
the call site). Threads through `load_wasm` exactly like `egress`.
Available only with the `wasm` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: search_path
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Add a directory to search for plugin dylibs.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_policy
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Set the load policy (Strict or Lenient).

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: require_signature
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Require plugins to have valid signatures.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: trusted_keys
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Set trusted Ed25519 public keys for signature verification.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: buffer_strategy
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Set the expected buffer strategy for validation.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: PluginHost builder and plugin discovery.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: builder
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: Create a new builder.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: discover
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Discover all valid plugins in the configured search paths.

Scans each path for both:
- dylib files (cdylib plugins, the existing path), and
- subdirectories containing a `package.toml` with `runtime = "python"`
(when the `python` feature is enabled).

Returns owned `PluginInfo` for every valid plugin found, with
`PluginInfo::runtime` distinguishing the two kinds.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: discover_cdylib
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment: PluginHost builder and plugin discovery.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: discover_package
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Discover a directory-based package (`package.toml`) and surface it by
runtime. Rust source packages are discovered via their built dylib (the
loadable artifact), not here, so they're skipped.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Load a specific plugin by name.

Searches all configured paths for a dylib containing a plugin
with the given name. Returns the loaded plugin ready for calling.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: find_python_package
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Find a python plugin package directory by name across the configured
search paths. The plugin name is matched against `package.toml`'s
`[package].name`. Returns the directory path on success.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_python
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Load a Python plugin package by name and validate it against the
supplied interface descriptor.

The caller passes the static `<TraitName>_PYTHON_DESCRIPTOR` emitted
by the interface crate's `#[plugin_interface]` macro ŌĆö that's the
out-of-band hint the loader needs to map method names to vtable
indices and to check the interface hash.

Available only when fidius-host is built with the `python` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_python_configured
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Load a **configured** Python plugin (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.4): serialize
`config` and bind it once via the module's
`__fidius_configure__(config) -> instance`; methods then run on the
configured instance. N differently-configured instances coexist.
Available only with the `python` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: find_wasm_package
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Find a WASM package directory by name across the search paths (matches
`package.toml` `[package].name` with `runtime = "wasm"`).

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_wasm
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Load a WASM component plugin package by name and validate it against the
supplied interface descriptor (the `<TraitName>_WASM_DESCRIPTOR` the
interface crate emits). Returns a unified [`crate::handle::PluginHandle`].

The component is sandboxed: WASI is wired into the `Linker` but the guest
gets a zero-grant `WasiCtx` (no FS preopens, no env, no sockets). The
capability allow-list in `[wasm].capabilities` is applied in T-0104.

Outbound HTTP is governed by the host's egress policy: a guest that
declares the `http` capability gets `wasi:http` only when the host was
given a policy (via [`PluginHostBuilder::egress`] or
[`Self::load_wasm_with_egress`]) ŌĆö otherwise it fails closed.

Available only with the `wasm` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_wasm_configured
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Load a **configured** WASM plugin (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.3): serialize
`config` and bind it once via the guest's `fidius-configure` export. The
component then runs on a persistent store, so methods dispatch on the
configured instance and config crosses the sandbox boundary exactly once.
Available only with the `wasm` feature.

crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.name: load_wasm_with_egress
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        descriptor: &'static fidius_core::wasm_descriptor::WasmInterfaceDescriptor,
        egress: impl crate::executor::wasm::EgressPolicy,
    ) -> Result<crate::handle::PluginHandle, LoadError>
crates/fidius-host/src/host.rs.doc_comment:
Like [`Self::load_wasm`] but with a **per-plugin** `wasi:http` egress
policy that overrides any host-wide default (FIDIUS-I-0027). This is the
right primitive for isolating connectors: a host-wide policy only sees the
outbound *request*, not which plugin issued it, so per-plugin policies are
how you bound connector A to one set of hosts and connector B to another.

crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: load_package_manifest
crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Load and validate a package manifest against a host-defined schema.

This is the primary entry point for host applications working with packages.
The type parameter `M` is the host's metadata schema ŌĆö if the manifest's
`[metadata]` section doesn't deserialize into `M`, this returns an error.

# Example

```ignore
#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct MySchema {
category: String,
min_host_version: String,
}

let manifest = load_package_manifest::<MySchema>(Path::new("./packages/blur/"))?;
assert_eq!(manifest.metadata.category, "image-processing");
```

crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: discover_packages
crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Discover packages in a directory.

Scans `dir` for subdirectories containing a `package.toml` file.
Returns the paths to each package directory found.

crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: verify_package
crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Verify a source package's signature against trusted public keys.

Recomputes the package digest from files on disk and verifies the
`package.sig` signature against the provided trusted keys.

# Errors

- `PackageError::SignatureNotFound` ŌĆö if `package.sig` doesn't exist
- `PackageError::SignatureInvalid` ŌĆö if no trusted key verifies the signature

crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: unpack_fid
crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Extract a `.fid` archive and validate its contents.

Delegates to [`fidius_core::package::unpack_package`], which applies strict
safety defaults: entries with `..` components, absolute paths, symlinks, or
hardlinks are rejected, and the cumulative decompressed size is capped to
guard against decompression bombs. Emits a `tracing::warn!` if the unpacked
package has no `package.sig`.

For archives that legitimately exceed the default caps, call
[`fidius_core::package::unpack_package_with_options`] directly.

# Example

```ignore
let pkg_dir = unpack_fid(Path::new("blur-filter-1.0.0.fid"), Path::new("./plugins/"))?;
let manifest = load_package_manifest::<MySchema>(&pkg_dir)?;
```

crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: build_package
crates/fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Build a package by running `cargo build` inside the package directory.

Returns the path to the compiled cdylib on success.

crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.name: sig_path_for
crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Compute the detached signature file path for a given file.

Appends `.sig` to the full filename (e.g., `foo.dylib` ŌåÆ `foo.dylib.sig`).

crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.name: verify_signature
crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Verify a plugin dylib's signature against trusted public keys.

Reads the dylib bytes and the detached `.sig` file, then verifies
the Ed25519 signature against each trusted key until one matches.

# Errors

- `LoadError::SignatureRequired` ŌĆö if the `.sig` file doesn't exist
- `LoadError::SignatureInvalid` ŌĆö if no trusted key verifies the signature

crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.name: verify_package_signature
crates/fidius-host/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Verify a **package** signature: `package.sig` in `dir`, an Ed25519 signature
over `package_digest(dir)` (the runtime-agnostic content hash), against any
trusted key. Used for package-based runtimes (Python, WASM) where the signed
artifact is the whole package directory, not a single dylib file.

`package_digest` excludes `*.sig`, so the digest is identical at sign and
verify time, and tampering with any package file (e.g. the `.wasm`
component) changes the digest and fails verification.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: ChunkStream
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.signature: { inner: Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = Result<Value, CallError>> + Send>> }
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Host-facing pull handle for a server-streaming plugin call.

A `futures::Stream` of decoded items. Pull with `.next().await`; the stream
ends after the producer's `END` frame, or yields one final `Err` and then
ends on an `ERROR`/abort/malformed frame. Dropping the handle cancels the
call ŌĆö the backend bridge observes the drop and tears its producer down.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: ChunkStream
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
turns that byte sequence into the item stream every backend bridge feeds.
[`ChunkStream::from_frame_bytes`] runs the terminal-frame state machine that
zero or more `ITEM` frames terminated by exactly one `END` or `ERROR`.
On the wire a stream is a sequence of [`Frame`]s (see [`fidius_core::frame`]):

## Wire

the producer down (D3 / REQ-002).
pulling (REQ-003). Dropping the `ChunkStream` drops the bridge, which tears
a backend bridge that fills a bounded channel blocks when the host stops
- **Backpressure & cancel are structural.** The host pulls with `.next().await`;
a tokio app, so the handle is awaited, not polled on a blocked thread.
`Result<Value, CallError>`. **Native async** (D1) ŌĆö every consuming host is

via its FFI iterator-handle ABI).

starts a server-streaming method and returns a [`ChunkStream`]. Implemented
- [`StreamExecutor`] ŌĆö the backend-side trait. `async fn call_streaming`

## The pieces (design decisions D1ŌĆōD3)

This module adds the *streaming* seam: one call in, an unbounded **pull**
The unary seam ([`crate::executor`]) carries one value in and one value out.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: new
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Wrap any item stream as a [`ChunkStream`]. Backends that already produce
`Result<Value, CallError>` items use this directly.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: from_frame_bytes
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Build a [`ChunkStream`] from a stream of raw, length-delimited frame
*bytes* (one encoded [`Frame`] per element ŌĆö the shape a serialized
backend's `next()` hands back: a WASM `list<u8>`, a cdylib buffer),
applying the terminal-frame state machine:

- `ITEM` ŌåÆ `decode_item(payload)` ŌåÆ `Ok(value)`, continue.
- `END` ŌåÆ stop (no item).
- `ERROR` ŌåÆ one `Err(CallError::Plugin)`, then stop.
- a malformed/truncated frame ŌåÆ one `Err(CallError::MalformedFrame)`, stop.
- an `ITEM` whose payload fails `decode_item` ŌåÆ that `Err`, then stop.
- the byte source ending **without** a terminal frame ŌåÆ one
`Err(CallError::StreamAborted)`, stop.

`decode_item` is caller-supplied because the ITEM payload is "one unary
return value": for cdylib that is concrete-type bincode the typed client
decodes via `wire::deserialize::<T>()` then `to_value`; for a `#[wire(raw)]`
stream it is the bytes themselves. (Vanilla bincode cannot reconstruct a
self-describing [`Value`] ŌĆö `deserialize_any` is unsupported ŌĆö so there is
deliberately no fixed "bytes ŌåÆ Value" decode here.) The self-describing
in-process backends (Python) skip framing entirely and use [`Self::new`].

After any error the stream is fused: subsequent polls yield `None`.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: from_frames
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Build a [`ChunkStream`] over a fixed, in-memory sequence of [`Frame`]s.
A convenience for tests and serialized-backend fixtures; runs the same
terminal-frame state machine as [`Self::from_frame_bytes`] with the same
caller-supplied `decode_item`.

crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.name: StreamExecutor
crates/fidius-host/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Backends whose typed boundary can produce a **server-streaming** result.

Sits beside [`crate::executor::ValueExecutor`]: that returns one [`Value`],
this returns a [`ChunkStream`] of them. Implemented by all three backends
(Python and WASM via self-describing values; cdylib via its FFI
iterator-handle ABI). `PluginHandle` routes a streaming method to
`call_streaming` exactly as it routes a unary one to `call`.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: PluginRuntimeKind
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Plugin runtime kind. Mirrors `fidius_core::package::PackageRuntime` and
surfaces it in the host-facing `PluginInfo`. Re-exported here so host
callers don't need a transitive `fidius-core` use.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: PluginInfo
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.signature: { name: String, interface_name: String, interface_hash: u64, interface_version: u32, capabilities: u64, buffer_strategy:...
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Owned metadata for a discovered or loaded plugin.

All data copied from FFI descriptor ŌĆö no raw pointers. `capabilities` and
`buffer_strategy` are cdylib-specific concepts; for python plugins they
take their default values (0 / `PluginAllocated`) and have no runtime
meaning.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: PluginInfo
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment: Owned metadata types for loaded plugins.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: is_cdylib
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment: True if this is a cdylib-backed plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: is_python
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment: True if this is a Python plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: is_wasm
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment: True if this is a WASM component plugin.

crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.name: LoadPolicy
crates/fidius-host/src/types.rs.doc_comment: Controls how strictly the host validates plugins.

crates/fidius-host/tests/wasm_egress_e2e.name: IFACE
crates/fidius-host/tests/wasm_egress_e2e.doc_comment:
embedder writes ŌĆö fidius ships none of this (mechanism, not policy).
The reference allow/deny policies here are exactly what the docs say an

- no policy / no capability ŌåÆ `wasi:http` isn't linked ŌåÆ fails closed.
- denied   ŌåÆ the policy refuses before dispatch; the guest gets an error;
- allowed  ŌåÆ the guest fetches the mock server's body;
every request through the embedder's `EgressPolicy`:
and makes an outbound GET. The host enforces the **two-key** gate and routes
component (`tests/wasm-fixtures/fetcher`) imports `wasi:http/outgoing-handler`
Capability-gated `wasi:http` egress E2E (FIDIUS-I-0027 / E2). A real WASM

crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.name: generate_wasm_adapter
crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.signature:
(
    trait_name: &Ident,
    instance_name: &Ident,
    methods: &[MethodInfo],
    config: Option<&Path>,
    crate_path: &Path,
    impl_type: &Type,
) -> TokenStream
crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.doc_comment:
Generate the WASM component auto-export adapter for `#[plugin_impl]`.

Emits, under `#[cfg(target_family = "wasm")]`, a `wit_bindgen::generate!`
invocation (inline WIT generated from the method signatures) plus a `Guest`
impl that forwards to the plugin's static instance, and `export!`. If any
method has a reference argument or a type outside the supported set, it
instead emits a wasm-gated `compile_error!` (a clear failure on wasm builds;
a no-op on cdylib/Python builds where the whole adapter is cfg'd out).

crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.name: collect_user_idents
crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.doc_comment:
Collect candidate user-type idents (non-primitive path leaves) from a type,
descending through `Vec`/`Option`/`Box` and `Result`'s ok type.

crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.name: gen_type
crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.doc_comment:
The wit-bindgen-generated type for an author type: identity for types holding
no user type (wit-bindgen uses the same Rust type), else the generated path
`exports::fidius::<iface>::<iface>::<T>`, recursing through `Vec`/`Option`.
