crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.name: wasm_unsupported
crates/fidius-macro/src/impl_macro.rs.doc_comment:
Emit a `#[cfg(target_family = "wasm")]`-gated `compile_error!` for a method
the WASM auto-export can't handle. On native (cdylib/Python) builds the cfg
is false, so this is a no-op there; on a wasm build it fails the compile with
a clear message instead of silently producing a component that exports nothing.

Strip fidius-specific helper attributes (`#[optional]`, `#[method_meta]`,
`#[trait_meta]`) from the trait and its methods so the emitted trait
compiles as a plain Rust trait definition.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: is_fidius_helper
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment:
capability bit constants, version/strategy constants, and a descriptor builder function.
Generates: the original trait, a `#[repr(C)]` vtable struct, interface hash constant,

Code generation for `#[plugin_interface]`.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: generate_interface
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment: Generate all code for a `#[plugin_interface]` invocation.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: generate_metadata
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment:
Emit the static metadata arrays for `#[method_meta]` and `#[trait_meta]`
attributes into the companion module. Emits:

- `__FIDIUS_METHOD_META_<NAME>: [MetaKv; N]` for each method with metadata
- `__FIDIUS_METHOD_META_TABLE: [MethodMetaEntry; method_count]` if any method has metadata
- `__FIDIUS_TRAIT_META: [MetaKv; N]` if any trait-level metadata exists

If no metadata is declared anywhere, emits nothing ŌĆö the descriptor
builder falls back to null pointers.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: generate_constants
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment: Generate interface hash, capability bit constants, version, and buffer strategy constants.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: generate_descriptor_builder
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment: Generate the descriptor builder function used by `#[plugin_impl]`.

crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.name: generate_client
crates/fidius-macro/src/interface.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a typed `{Trait}Client` struct that wraps a `PluginHandle` and
exposes named methods matching the trait signature. Eliminates raw
`call_method(index, ...)` boilerplate at host call sites.

Emission is gated with `#[cfg(feature = "host")]` so plugin cdylibs that
don't enable the `host` feature pay zero cost (no `fidius-host`, no
`libloading` in the dep tree). Host applications enable the feature on
the interface crate to receive the generated Client type.

Uniform tuple encoding matches the plugin-side shim (see
`impl_macro.rs::generate_shims`): args are always serialized as a tuple
`(arg1, arg2, ...)` ŌĆö for zero args this is `()`; for one arg `(arg,)`.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: parse_meta_attrs
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment:
Parse all `#[method_meta("k", "v")]` or `#[trait_meta("k", "v")]`
attributes with the given name from an attribute list into a `Vec<MetaKvAttr>`.
Validates string-literal only, non-empty keys, no duplicate keys, and
rejects keys in the reserved `fidius.*` namespace.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: parse_optional_attr
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment: Parse an `#[optional(since = N)]` attribute, if present.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: parse_wire_attr
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment:
Parse a `#[wire(raw)]` attribute, if present. Returns `true` when raw wire
mode is opted in, `false` otherwise. Any other `wire(...)` form is a
compile-time error.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: is_vec_u8
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment: Return `true` if the given type is `Vec<u8>`.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: result_ok_type
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment: Extract the first type parameter of `Result<_, _>`, if `ty` is a Result.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: validate_raw_method_signature
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment:
Validate that a method flagged `#[wire(raw)]` has a supported signature:
exactly one `Vec<u8>` argument, and returns either `Vec<u8>` or
`Result<Vec<u8>, _>`. Returns a helpful error otherwise.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: stream_item_type
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment:
Return the per-item type `T` if `ty` is a `Stream<T>` (i.e. its final path
segment is `Stream` with exactly one angle-bracketed type argument). Matches
`fidius::Stream<T>`, `crate::fidius::Stream<T>`, or a bare `Stream<T>` ŌĆö the
detection keys on the segment name, since the marker is written explicitly
(FIDIUS-I-0026, D4). Returns `None` for any other type.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: build_signature_string
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.signature:
(
    method: &TraitItemFn,
    wire_raw: bool,
    stream_item: Option<&Type>,
) -> String
crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.doc_comment:
Build the canonical signature string for a method.

Delegates the format to `fidius_core::hash::signature_string` so the
proc macro and any other tooling (e.g. `fidius python-stub`) share one
source of truth. The `!raw` (raw-wire) and `!stream` (server-streaming)
markers are part of that shared format.

For a streaming method (`-> fidius::Stream<T>`) the canonical return type is
the per-item type `T` (passed in `stream_item`), plus the `!stream` marker ŌĆö
so `read:->Row!stream` hashes distinctly from a unary `read:->Row`.

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: extract_arg_names

crates/fidius-macro/src/ir.rs.name: extract_return_type

crates/fidius-macro/src/lib.rs.name: plugin_interface
plugin_impl
derive_wit_type
crates/fidius-macro/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Mark a `struct`/`enum` as usable in a WASM plugin interface (FIDIUS-I-0023).

This is a **marker** derive: it emits no code. The `fidius wit` generator
(run from `build.rs`) keys on the `#[derive(WitType)]` attribute when it
parses the crate source, mapping the struct to a WIT `record` (named fields)
or the enum to a WIT `variant` (unit / single-field cases) and emitting the
generatedŌåöauthor conversions the wasm adapter uses. The same type continues
to cross the cdylib/Python boundary via serde, unchanged.

```ignore
#[derive(WitType, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct Point { pub x: i32, pub y: i32 }
```

naming convention. `BytePipe` ŌåÆ `byte-pipe`, `echo_bytes` ŌåÆ `echo-bytes`.

crates/fidius-python/build.rs.name: main
crates/fidius-python/build.rs.doc_comment:
Build script: configure PyO3 cfg flags and emit a runtime rpath so the
embedded Python shared library is found at process launch.

Without this, macOS framework Python builds crash on first call with:

```text
dyld: Library not loaded: @rpath/Python3.framework/Versions/3.x/Python3
```

Mirrors the cloacina-build pattern (which exists for the same reason).

crates/fidius-python/src/error.rs.name: pyerr_to_plugin_error
crates/fidius-python/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Convert a `PyErr` into a `PluginError`, preserving class name, message,
and a formatted traceback in `details`.

Acquires the GIL internally, so callers can pass a `PyErr` they captured
outside `Python::with_gil` (the typical case for `?` propagation).

crates/fidius-python/src/error.rs.name: format_traceback
crates/fidius-python/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Format a Python traceback into a plain string by calling
`traceback.format_tb(tb)` and joining the result. Best-effort: returns an
empty string on internal failure rather than recursively raising.

crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.name: call_typed
crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Typed dispatch.

`input_bincode` is the bincode-encoded args tuple ŌĆö the same byte
payload the cdylib path would receive. We use bincode here only
because every other fidius caller does; on the way into Python we
pivot through `serde_json::Value` (so the host's `I: Serialize` works
for any type the macro accepts).

crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.name: call_typed_json
crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Typed dispatch where the input is already JSON-serialised (the
host's `serde_json::to_vec(&input)`). Returns JSON bytes the caller
`serde_json::from_slice::<O>` decodes.

crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.name: call_streaming_start
crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Start a server-streaming call (FIDIUS-I-0026). Calls the method to obtain
its iterator/generator and wraps it in a [`crate::stream::PythonStream`]
the host then pumps. Input is JSON like [`Self::call_typed_json`];
streaming methods use the typed (non-raw) path.

crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Raw dispatch ŌĆö pass bytes in, get bytes out, no encoding.

crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.name: build_call_args
crates/fidius-python/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Build positional args for `callable.call(...)` from a JSON value.

The host's typed encoding is a tuple `(arg1, arg2, ...)` ŌĆö this surfaces
as a JSON array. We unpack each element as a positional Python arg so
`def greet(name)` works rather than `def greet((name,))`. Non-array
values (degenerate case for zero-arg methods that produce JSON `null`)
dispatch as zero-arg calls.

crates/fidius-python/src/interpreter.rs.name: ensure_initialized
crates/fidius-python/src/interpreter.rs.doc_comment:
Idempotent: ensure the embedded Python interpreter is initialised.

`auto-initialize` already brings it up on the first `Python::with_gil`
call, so this function is mostly a documented entry point and a tracing
hook for diagnosing "did Python actually start up" issues. Calling it
repeatedly is cheap.

crates/fidius-python/src/lib.rs.name: error
crates/fidius-python/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Python plugin runtime for Fidius.

This crate embeds CPython into the host process via PyO3 and (in later
tasks) exposes a `PluginHandle` whose dispatcher calls into a loaded
Python module. Hosts opt into Python plugin support by depending on this
crate (typically through `fidius-host`'s `python` feature flag).

At this stage the crate provides only the foundation: shared interpreter
initialisation and Python-exception-to-`PluginError` conversion. The
loader, dispatcher, and packaging integration land in subsequent tasks
under FIDIUS-I-0020.

crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.name: load_python_plugin
crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.doc_comment:
Load a Python plugin package against a static interface descriptor.

`package_dir` must point at an unpacked Python plugin directory (the
thing `unpack_package` returns or that lives next to a `package.toml`
during local dev).

crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.name: load_python_plugin_configured
crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.signature:
(
    package_dir: &Path,
    descriptor: &'static PythonInterfaceDescriptor,
    config: &serde_json::Value,
) -> Result<PythonPluginHandle, PythonLoadError>
crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.doc_comment:
Load a **configured** Python plugin instance (FIDIUS-A-0006 / CI.4): import the
module, call its module-level `__fidius_configure__(config) -> instance` with
the deserialized config, and bind methods on the returned instance ŌĆö so the
config is bound once and N differently-configured instances coexist (each is a
distinct object). The module must export `__fidius_configure__`.

crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.name: prepend_sys_path
crates/fidius-python/src/loader.rs.doc_comment:
Prepend `<dir>/vendor` and `<dir>` to `sys.path` if not already present.
Both are pushed at index 0 so they shadow anything else with the same
module name ŌĆö important for the vendored-deps story.

crates/fidius-python/src/stream.rs.name: next
crates/fidius-python/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Advance one item. Holds the GIL only for the duration of this call, so a
slow downstream consumer never pins the interpreter.

crates/fidius-python/src/stream.rs.name: cancel
crates/fidius-python/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Cancel the stream: run the generator's cleanup by calling `close()`,
which raises `GeneratorExit` inside the generator so its `finally`/
context-manager `__exit__` runs (closing DB cursors, releasing handles).
A no-op for iterables without `close` (e.g. a plain `list_iterator`).

crates/fidius-python/tests/loader_e2e.rs.name: fresh_descriptor
crates/fidius-python/tests/loader_e2e.rs.doc_comment:
Make a `'static` interface descriptor with a unique name so each test
gets its own slot in Python's `sys.modules` cache. Tests share the
embedded interpreter, so a fixed module name across tests would mean
the second-and-later loads silently reuse the first test's module
(the shared-`sys.modules` constraint we ship as a documented feature).

crates/fidius-test/src/dylib.rs.doc_comment:
Start building a dylib fixture for the plugin crate at `plugin_dir`.

`plugin_dir` must contain a `Cargo.toml` with `crate-type = ["cdylib"]`.
The resulting fixture caches the build across the current test binary
process; subsequent calls with the same `plugin_dir` return the cached
fixture without re-running cargo.

signing
stream

crates/fidius-test/src/signing.rs.name: fixture_keypair_with_seed
crates/fidius-test/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Deterministic Ed25519 keypair derived from `seed` repeated 32 times.

Use different seeds across tests that need distinct keys (e.g., to verify
that a wrong-key signature is rejected).

crates/fidius-test/src/signing.rs.name: sign_dylib
crates/fidius-test/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Sign a plugin dylib in place by writing a detached `.sig` file alongside it.

The signature file uses the same naming convention as `fidius sign` ŌĆö
appends `.sig` to the full filename (e.g., `foo.dylib` ŌåÆ `foo.dylib.sig`).

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: StreamSink
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
The destination side of a pipe: a consumer `pump` hands each item to, in
pull order. Deliberately tiny ŌĆö a real destination plugin presents whatever
richer surface it likes; this is only what composition tests need.

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: stream_of
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
An in-memory source over a fixed item sequence. **Test-only by nature** ŌĆö
you would never construct a stream from a `Vec` in production; that it is
useful only in tests is exactly why the whole module is test-tier.

Yields each value as `Ok`, then a clean end of stream.

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: collect
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
Drain a stream to a `Vec`, stopping at ŌĆö and returning ŌĆö the first error.
The single-plugin unit-test idiom: `collect(plugin.process(stream_of(rows)))`.

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: pump
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
The reference pull-loop wiring a producer stream to a [`StreamSink`].

Pull-paced: exactly one item is awaited at a time, so a slow sink naturally
backpressures the producer (the next item is not pulled until the sink has
accepted the current one). Stops at the first error from either side ŌĆö
producer error or sink rejection ŌĆö and returns it; on a clean end of stream
returns `Ok(())`. This is the ~10 lines you would copy into production and
then grow your own retries/observability around.

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: CollectSink
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
A [`StreamSink`] that records everything it accepts ŌĆö for asserting on the
far end of a `pump`.

crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.name: CollectSink
crates/fidius-test/src/stream.rs.doc_comment:
See ADR FIDIUS-A-0004 ("Streaming as Mechanism, Not Protocol").
orchestrator; orchestration (scheduling, retries, observability) is yours.
loop** ŌĆö fidius ships the typed pipe (the [`ChunkStream`] primitive), not an
but its API may change at any time. **In production, write your own pump
is *correct* (pull-paced backpressure, stop-and-surface on the first error)
so the canonical composition pattern is readable in one place. Its behaviour
It exists so tests can compose streams without re-deriving the same loop, and
**This module is deliberately NOT part of fidius's semver-stable surface.**

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: Generated
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.signature: { interface_name: String, iface_kebab: String, user_types: Vec<String>, wit: String, conversions: String }
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment: The product of generating from a plugin crate's source.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: generate
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Generate WIT + conversions from a crate's source string (`lib.rs`). Inline
modules (`mod m { .. }`) are walked; external `mod m;` files cannot be read
from a bare string ŌĆö use [`generate_from_path`] (the `build.rs` helper does).

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: generate_from_path
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Like [`generate`], but reads `lib_rs` and follows external `mod m;` files
(resolving `m.rs` / `m/mod.rs`), so `#[derive(WitType)]` types and the
`#[plugin_interface]` trait may live in submodules.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: Collected
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.signature: { structs: Vec<(Vec<String>, syn::ItemStruct)>, enums: Vec<(Vec<String>, syn::ItemEnum)>, the_trait: Option<syn::ItemTra...
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
`#[derive(WitType)]` types (tagged with their Rust module path) + the
`#[plugin_interface]` trait, gathered across the module tree.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: collect
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.signature:
(
    items: &[Item],
    mod_path: &[String],
    dir: Option<&std::path::Path>,
    acc: &mut Collected,
) -> Result<(), String>
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Recursively gather items, descending into inline `mod m { .. }` and (when
`dir` is `Some`) external `mod m;` files (`m.rs` / `m/mod.rs`).

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: assemble
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment: Build the `.wit` + conversions from the collected items.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: author_path
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment: `crate::<mod::path>::<Name>` ŌĆö the author-side path for a type at `mod_path`.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: render_conversions
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.signature:
(
    iface_kebab: &str,
    structs: &[(Vec<String>, syn::ItemStruct)],
    enums: &[(Vec<String>, syn::ItemEnum)],
    known: &BTreeSet<String>,
) -> String
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Render `From` impls (both directions) between each user type and its
wit-bindgen-generated mirror. Emitted into the adapter module, where the
generated types live (flat) at `exports::fidius::<iface>::<iface>::<Type>`
and the author types at `crate::<mod::path>::<Type>`.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: conv_expr
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Conversion expression for a field/payload `access` of type `ty`. Identity
(move) when the type holds no user type (generated and author types are then
identical); otherwise `.into()` (user type), or a `map`/`into_iter().map()`
recursing through `Option`/`Vec`. Symmetric ŌĆö works generatedŌåÆauthor and
authorŌåÆgenerated, since `From` is generated both ways. Public so the macro's
adapter reuses the exact same boundary conversions.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: contains_user_type
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
Whether `ty` is, or contains (through `Vec`/`Option`/`Box`), a user type in
`known`. Public so the macro can classify args/returns.

crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.name: single_generic
crates/fidius-wit/src/generate.rs.doc_comment:
the `fidius wit` CLI, which read the source files.
can't see external type definitions, so this runs from a `build.rs` helper and
generatedŌåöauthor `From` conversions the wasm adapter includes. A proc-macro
document (records/variants + funcs) and (b) the Rust source for
every `#[derive(WitType)]` `struct`/`enum`, and produces (a) a complete `.wit`

This is a plain library (not a proc-macro), so `fidius-macro`, the `build.rs`

(enums), referenced by their kebab-case name.

`Option<T>`, and `Result<T, PluginError>` map directly; user types that

Maps the Rust types in a `#[plugin_interface]` to WIT, per

WIT generation for the Fidius WASM backend (FIDIUS-I-0023).

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: to_kebab_case
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Convert a Rust identifier (CamelCase or snake_case) to kebab-case, the WIT
naming convention. `BytePipe` ŌåÆ `byte-pipe`, `echo_bytes` ŌåÆ `echo-bytes`.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: result_ok_type
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment: Extract the `T` from `Result<T, _>`, if `ty` is a `Result`.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: WitMethod
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.signature: { name: String, params: Vec<(String, String)>, ret: Option<String>, stream_item: Option<String> }
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment: One method projected to WIT (already-mapped strings).

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: stream_item_type
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
If `ty` is `fidius::Stream<T>` (final path segment `Stream`, exactly one type
argument), return `T`. The server-streaming marker (FIDIUS-I-0026, D4). Public
so the macro's WASM adapter shares the same detection.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: wit_type_with
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Map a Rust argument/return type to its WIT spelling, where `known` holds the
Rust identifiers of `#[derive(WitType)]` user types (mapped to their
kebab-case record/variant name). Returns `Err(msg)` for unsupported types.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: rust_type_to_wit
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Primitive/std-only mapping (no user types) ŌĆö the form `fidius-macro` uses for
the descriptor/method tables.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: return_to_wit_with
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Map a method's return type to an optional WIT return, with user types in
`known`. `Result<T, PluginError>` ŌåÆ `result<T, plugin-error>` (or
`result<_, plugin-error>` for `T = ()`); `()`/none ŌåÆ no return.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: return_to_wit
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment: Primitive/std-only return mapping (no user types).

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: struct_to_record
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Render a `record <name> { ... }` WIT block from a Rust struct (named fields
only). Field types are mapped with `known` so they may reference other user
types.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: enum_to_wit
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Render a Rust enum to WIT: a `variant <name> { ... }` plus any **synthetic
records** for struct-shaped cases. Returns `(synthetic_records, variant)`.

Case shapes: unit ŌåÆ `case`; single tuple field ŌåÆ `case(type)`; named fields
(`Case { .. }`) ŌåÆ a synthesized `record <enum>-<case>` and `case(<enum>-<case>)`.
A multi-field *tuple* case is rejected: WIT cases take one payload, and a
multi-field tuple serializes as a sequence (not a record) via serde, so it
can't round-trip ŌĆö use a struct variant `Case { .. }` instead.

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: render_wit_full
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Render a complete `.wit` document: package + interface (the `plugin-error`
record, any user `type_defs` (records/variants), the funcs, and the
`fidius-interface-hash` carrier) + the `<iface>-plugin` world. `type_defs`
are pre-rendered (see [`struct_to_record`] / [`enum_to_variant`]).

crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.name: render_wit
crates/fidius-wit/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Convenience: render a WIT document with no user type defs (the primitives-only
form `fidius-macro` emits inline today).

python/fidius/_errors.py.name: PluginError
python/fidius/_errors.py.doc_comment:
Structured plugin error that round-trips to the host with its fields intact.

    Parameters
    ----------
    code:
        Short machine-readable error code (e.g. ``"BAD_INPUT"``).
    message:
        Human-readable explanation.
    details:
        Optional dict of structured context. Must be JSON-serialisable.

python/fidius/_errors.py.name: __init__

python/fidius/_registry.py.name: method
python/fidius/_registry.py.doc_comment:
Register *func* under its ``__name__`` as a fidius plugin method.

    The registry is scoped per-module: two plugins can both expose
    ``@method def process()`` without clashing. Raises :class:`ValueError`
    if the *same* module registers two functions with the same name.

python/fidius/_registry.py.name: get_method
python/fidius/_registry.py.signature: def get_method(name: str, module: str | None = None) -> Callable
python/fidius/_registry.py.doc_comment:
Look up a previously-registered method.

    If *module* is given, looks for the entry registered against that
    module specifically. If *module* is ``None``, searches across all
    modules and raises if the name is ambiguous (registered in more than
    one module). Raises :class:`KeyError` if unknown.

python/fidius/_registry.py.name: list_methods
python/fidius/_registry.py.signature: def list_methods(module: str | None = None) -> list[str]
python/fidius/_registry.py.doc_comment:
Return the sorted list of registered method names.

    If *module* is given, restricts to that module's methods. Otherwise
    returns the union across all modules.

python/fidius/_registry.py.name: reset_registry
python/fidius/_registry.py.doc_comment: Clear the registry. Intended for use in tests; not part of the public API.
